title: requests — rollout flags
description: Rollout / change-management flags for THIS module. Distinct from `features.json` (tenant-facing catalogue entries). Every material change ships behind a flag per SOC 2 CC8.1.

flags.description: Turns off ALL submit endpoints without a redeploy. Incident-response only — flipping off returns 503 to every POST /access-requests. Existing pending requests continue through approval + materialisation normally.
The `access.request_hint_on_denial` middleware. On by default. Kill switch on if we ever hit a 403-decoration pathology (e.g. a bad registry entry causing false-positive hints).

AccessRequestGrantMaterializer runs inline in the ApprovalExecuted listener chain (synchronous with commit). Setting to `off` reroutes materialisation through a queued job (`MaterializeGrantJob`, not shipped yet). Only for use if grant creation ever becomes slow enough to stall the approval commit.

AccessRequestApproved notification fires on the ApprovalGranted event (BEFORE the grant is materialised). Body language: 'you should now be able to access X' (optimistic). Setting to `off` defers the notification to AccessRequestGrantIssued so it only sends AFTER the grant exists. Trade-off: certainty vs latency.
AccessRequestApproverResolver consults active `role_delegations` and swaps delegated approvers into the resolved set. Off by default in dev environments to make delegation debugging easier.

When the approver-selector returns an empty set (no admin role in the tenant, no matching template), fall back to the resource's `created_by` user as the sole approver. Off = raise INVALID_APPROVER_CONFIGURATION instead. Recommended ON in production for graceful degradation; OFF in dev to surface misconfigurations early.
SDUI bulk-approve control in the pending-my-review list. Ships to enterprise-tier canary tenants first; broader rollout gated on real-world data.

cohorts.name: internal-compliance-team
cohorts.predicate: user.email ENDS WITH '@academorix.com' AND user.groups CONTAINS 'compliance'

flags.description: Enhanced audit-report format with per-resource-type breakdowns + time-to-decision histograms. Off until the compliance team signs off on the shape.

retirement_policy.description: A flag lives at most 90 days after reaching 100% GA. After 90d, either the flag is deleted OR converts to a `kill-switch`. Prevents flag-registry sprawl.
